children.name: Gadiculus
children.description: Gadiculus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Gadiculus argenteus
children.description: Gadiculus argenteus, or the silvery pout, is a species of cod found in the Northeast Atlantic region.

children.name: Arctogadus
children.description: Arctogadus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Arctogadus borisovi
children.description: The East Siberian cod, also known as the toothed cod, is an Arctic fish closely similar to the Arctic cod Arctogadus glacialis and also related to true cods. It has been differentiated in appearance from the Arctic cod by having pronounced chin barbel. Their sides and back are dark olive and the belly are light grey with dark spots.

children.name: Arctogadus glacialis
children.description: Category:Taxa named by Wilhelm Peters Category:Fish described in 1872 Category:Gadidae.

children.name: Raniceps
children.description: Raniceps — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Raniceps raninus
children.description: Raniceps raninus, the tadpole fish, is a species of gadiform fish native to the northeast Atlantic Ocean around the coasts of France, Ireland, and the United Kingdom and the North Sea.

children.name: Micromesistius
children.description: Micromesistius — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Micromesistius australis
children.description: The southern blue whiting is a codfish of the genus Micromesistius, found in the southern oceans with temperatures between 3 and 7 °C, at depths of 50 to 900 m. Its length is commonly between 30 and 60 cm, with a maximum length of 90 cm. Maximum weight is at least 1350 g.

children.name: Micromesistius environmental sample
children.description: Micromesistius environmental sample — a species in the genus Micromesistius, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.

children.name: Micromesistius poutassou
children.description: The blue whiting one of the two species in the genus Micromesistius in the order of Gadiformes, which also contains cod, haddock, whiting, and pollock. It is common in the northeast Atlantic Ocean from Morocco to Iceland and Spitsbergen. It also occurs in the northern parts of the Mediterranean, where it may be locally abundant.

children.name: Boreogadus
children.description: Boreogadus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Microgadus
children.description: Microgadus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Microgadus proximus
children.description: Microgadus proximus, also commonly known as Pacific tomcod, is a type of cod fish found in North American coastal waters from the southeastern Bering Sea to central California.

children.name: Microgadus tomcod
children.description: Microgadus tomcod, also commonly known as frostfish, Atlantic tomcod or winter cod, is a type of cod found in North American coastal waters from the Gulf of St.

children.name: Trisopterus
children.description: Trisopterus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Trisopterus capelanus
children.description: Trisopterus capelanus is a fish species belonging to the cod family (Gadidae).

children.name: Trisopterus esmarkii
children.description: Trisopterus esmarkii, the Norway pout, is a species of fish in the cod family.

children.name: Trisopterus luscus
children.description: Trisopterus luscus ( ; most commonly known as pouting, but also called bib, pout whiting or pout) is a sea fish belonging to the cod family (Gadidae).

children.name: Pollachius
children.description: Pollachius — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Pollachius environmental sample
children.description: Pollachius environmental sample — a species in the genus Pollachius, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.

children.name: Pollachius pollachius
children.description: Pollachius pollachius is a species of marine fish in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Pollachius sp.Cas-2009
children.description: Pollachius sp.Cas-2009 — a species in the genus Pollachius, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.

children.name: Pollachius virens
children.description: {{Speciesbox | name = Saithe | image = Pollachius virens 01.jpg | image_caption = At Ozeaneum | image2 = | taxon = Pollachius virens | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | synonyms = }} Pollachius virens is a species of marine fish in the genus Pollachius.

children.name: Merlangius
children.description: Merlangius — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Merlangius merlangus
children.description: Merlangius merlangus, commonly known as whiting or merling, is an important food fish in the eastern North Atlantic Ocean and the northern Mediterranean, western Baltic, and Black Sea. In Anglophonic countries outside the whiting's natural range, the name "whiting" has been applied to various other species of fish.

children.name: Melanogrammus
children.description: Melanogrammus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Melanogrammus aeglefinus
children.description: The haddock is a saltwater ray-finned fish from the family Gadidae, the true cods. It is the only species in the monotypic genus Melanogrammus. It is found in the North Atlantic Ocean and associated seas, where it is an important species for fisheries, especially in northern Europe, where it is marketed fresh, frozen and smoked; smoked varieties include the Finnan haddie and the Arbroath smokie.

children.name: Gadus
children.description: Gadus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Gadus chalcogrammus
children.description: Category:Taxa named by Peter Simon Pallas.

children.name: Gadus finnmarchicus
children.description: Gadus finnmarchicus — a species in the genus Gadus, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.

children.name: Gadus macrocephalus
children.description: The Pacific cod is a species of ray-finned fish in the family Gadidae. It is a bottom-dwelling fish found in the northern Pacific Ocean, mainly on the continental shelf and upper slopes, to depths of about 900 m (3,000 ft). It can grow to a length of a meter or so and is found in large schools.

children.name: Gadus morhua
children.description: The Atlantic cod is a fish of the family Gadidae, widely consumed by humans. It is also commercially known as cod or codling.

children.name: Gadus sp.MT08394
children.description: Gadus sp.MT08394 — a species in the genus Gadus, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.

children.name: Gadus sp.Opc-2017
children.description: Gadus sp.Opc-2017 — a species in the genus Gadus, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.

children.name: Gadus spec.
children.description: Gadus spec. — a species in the genus Gadus, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.

children.name: Eleginus
children.description: Eleginus — a genus of cod & allies in the family Gadidae.

children.name: Eleginus navaga
children.description: Eleginus navaga — a species in the genus Eleginus, family Gadidae. Also known as cod & allies.
